raw_consumer_statement: I recently filed a dispute via phone for my Home Depot credit card. I was unable to access the process in my account online to do so. I have been charged late fees and interest on a balance that is in dispute without having received a timely notice. I sent documentation by fax ( only fax and portal options were provided when I filed by phone ) which was never acknowledged. I am told dispute was resolved, but have not gotten a letter stating same although I am told my account has now been updated ( again ) and payment is deemed late ( and subject to interest, fees, etc ). days after I am told letter was produced. I requested the cardmember agreement by phone and was told to send a secure message and it could be mailed to me. Its not available online. I finally located it in my files- and my cardmember agreement does NOT include a dispute resolution process. It only includes arbitration for collection and non-payment. I referred to my actual billing statement- It also does not have ANY information on what to do if your bill is wrong. I was under the impression that billing error notice must be included on credit card statements at a minimum ( all my other ones have it... ). I have no idea what my rights are in this matter, and it is a serious matter.
